consentRequirements: Author consent is presumed via voluntary contribution. Document if explicit consent was obtained.

Community attribution requires community-level consent through the appropriate governance body (band council, tribal council, Inuit organization). The community name must identify the specific nation or community — not a pan-Indigenous label. OCAP Ownership principle applies: the knowledge belongs to the community, and attribution alone does not constitute permission to use restricted knowledge.

exampleAttribution.name: Garden River First Nation
exampleAttribution.role: Community knowledge partner and content reviewer

consentRequirements: Elder attribution requires documented individual consent. Nation field is required — always use the specific nation name (e.g., 'Inuit (Kivalliq)' not 'Indigenous elder'). If the elder prefers not to be named, use 'Inuit elder, [region]' with their explicit consent for that phrasing.

Tradition-bearer attribution requires documented individual consent. For Indigenous tradition bearers, nation field is required. For Appalachian tradition bearers, region and community context is appropriate.

exampleAttribution.name: Merle Hensley
exampleAttribution.role: Appalachian basket-weaving tradition bearer, Ashe County, North Carolina

consentRequirements: Institutional attribution should reference any permissions, licenses, or endorsement documentation. For Indigenous institutions (ITK, FNIGC, tribal governments), note the specific authorization document.

exampleAttribution.name: Inuit Tapiriit Kanatami (ITK)
exampleAttribution.role: National Inuit Strategy on Research (NISR) framework guidance
